1 # Security Policy 2 3 ## Reporting a Vulnerability 4 5 The SkiaSharp Chart Engine team takes security vulnerabilities seriously. If you discover a security vulnerability, **please do not open a public issue**. Instead, use one of the following methods to report it confidentially. 6 7 ### Reporting Methods 8 9 1. **GitHub Private Vulnerability Reporting (Recommended)** 10 - Visit: https://github.com/vladyslav-zaiets/skiasharp-chart-engine/security/advisories/new 11 - This allows you to report directly within GitHub's secure system 12 13 2. **Email** 14 - Send details to: rutova2@gmail.com 15 - Include "SECURITY" in the subject line 16 17 ### What to Include 18 19 Please provide: 20 - Description of the vulnerability 21 - Steps to reproduce (if applicable) 22 - Potential impact 23 - Suggested fix (if you have one) 24 - Your contact information (optional) 25 26 ## Response Timeline 27 28 We are committed to addressing security vulnerabilities promptly: 29 30 - **48 hours**: Initial acknowledgment of your report 31 - **1 week**: Initial security assessment and plan 32 - **30 days**: Target for patch release (may vary based on complexity) 33 34 ## Supported Versions 35 36 Security patches will be provided for: 37 38 | Version | Status | End of Support | 39 |---------|--------|----------------| 40 | 2.0.x | Supported | TBD | 41 | 1.x | Security fixes only | TBD | 42 43 ## Security Best Practices for Users 44 45 When using SkiaSharp Chart Engine in production: 46 47 1. Keep your .NET runtime and dependencies up to date 48 2. Use the latest version of SkiaSharp Chart Engine 49 3. Validate and sanitize all user inputs before passing to the chart engine 50 4. Run applications with minimal required permissions 51 5. Monitor for security advisories 52 53 ## Contact 54 55 For security matters, contact the maintainers at the email address above. For other inquiries, please use standard GitHub Issues.
